checklist_skip_note: Phase 1+2 skipped for this PROMOTED iteration per Decide outcome 2 — the shadow's inputs ARE a Phase 1+2+3 result. This is the one sanctioned Phase 1+2 skip on iter >= 2; diff_profile.checklist_skipped records it as intentional.
phase3_note: A promoted iteration takes the shadow's new findings as its Phase 3 findings (recorded on iter-1's shadow block) and goes straight to Step 2.5 -> Step 3; it dispatches no fleet of its own.

fix_decisions.note: override ground had no completed-round precondition; guarded at BOTH the write boundary (record-override refuses) and the read boundary (_valid_override re-checks, so a hand-edited state file cannot smuggle it). Reproduced at the CLI before fixing: init -> record-override -> eligible=yes ground=override -> emit-body emitted the body at rc=0.

digest-unbound override failed open on a file-arm epoch. SKILL.md:242 already required --draft-file on EVERY record-override call on a file-arm epoch; the tool now enforces it rather than trusting prose — which is issue #546's whole premise.

token-binding documented_falsehood. Fixed the CODE to match the documented contract (rather than weakening the docs): the override ground now keys on the answering digest when bound. Verified byte-distinct drafts mint distinct tokens; the changeset/docs claims are now true.
init --force wiped forward-only creation tamper evidence; added the missing third guard with the exemption set byte-identical to its two siblings.

fix_decisions.claim_text: The illegal-transition vs environmental-failure discriminator the SKILL.md routes on is unparseable free-form English; the nine _TRANSITION_REASONS tokens are dead vocabulary, never emitted.
fix_decisions.evidence: Real and correctly graded — I did not demote it. Not fixed in-loop because the fix is a design change, not a repair: it means adding a machine-readable failure taxonomy (a distinct exit code or a reason=<token> field) across every _fail call site, and those breadcrumbs are pinned by name across several existing run.sh blocks. That is a change the issue should authorize, not one a fix loop should smuggle into a PR whose scope is the lifecycle extraction. It is NOT a REJECT driver (verdict_severity_threshold=critical, and it is Important), it fails closed today (an illegal transition exits non-zero with a named breadcrumb; only the CLASS attribution is left to prose), and iteration 1's silent-failure-hunter independently raised the same concern from the SKILL.md side — two independent passes agreeing it is real. Recorded here for a follow-up issue rather than parked silently. Revisit condition: file the follow-up before any further prose is added that routes on this distinction.

fix_decisions.note: the fix-delta gate proved MY OWN token row vacuous: two slugs meant two random nonces, so the tokens differed regardless of the key and the row stayed GREEN with the key fix reverted. Rewritten to one slug/one nonce with two digest-bound overrides at the same ordinal; mutation-proven RED.

the gate caught a documented_falsehood in my own it6 fixture comment — I claimed the row 'passed on the wrong breadcrumb', but pre-delta it passed on the RIGHT one; the fixture needed changing because the new preconditions refuse first. Corrected.

the re-gate found my docstring claimed BOTH preconditions are re-checked at the read boundary while only precondition 1 was pinned there — the read guard could be reverted with all 11 rows green. Added the symmetric (2b) row; mutation-proven RED (emit rc=0 emitting NEVER AUDITED BYTES without the guard).

trivial + clearly correct: replaced the draft_digest falsy-coercion with an explicit `is not None` test (unreachable today, but its correctness rested on a distant invariant in a repo with an explicit valid-falsy rule).

fix_decisions.claim_text: An unbound override on a file-arm epoch refuses as stale-override, but nothing went stale — it was never bound, so it was never compared. The module's own comment states that standard for the sibling case.
fix_decisions.evidence: parked-origin: below-threshold. Raised by three independent gate passes and real, but Suggestion-tier: it fails CLOSED (the refusal is correct; only the diagnosis is misattributed) and is now unreachable through the CLI because the write guard refuses that shape — only a pre-delta or hand-edited state file reaches it. Naming the honest cause needs a new token in the closed _ELIGIBILITY_REASONS vocabulary, which issue #546's AC also enumerates and pins per state class — not trivial, so Step 3 item 2 says leave it. Mitigated in the meantime: row (2b) greps '^eligible=no ' rather than the exact reason, so it stays green and correct if the token is later corrected.

fix_decisions.claim_text: evaluate_triggers docstring omits the third branch forcing T2 true on a no-verdict outcome
_validate does not assert the outcome/pending mutual-exclusivity invariant, nor re-check the user-decline surface requirement at load

stale_prose_check.rows: no STALE rows at post-fix HEAD

skipped_checks.reason: working tree dirty at the pre-commit run (this check grades committed HEAD). It ran GREEN on the clean committed tree — the independent fix-delta re-gate over fd07676c reported the suite as '8532 passed, 0 failed, no skips', i.e. the gate executed for real rather than self-skipping.

test_first_gate.decision: automated-test arm: each fix adds behavior a test can exercise (a refusal, an exit code, a token value)
test_first_gate.evidence: RED-before-GREEN observed on the real code rather than attested: each exploit was first reproduced end-to-end at the CLI (Critical 1: eligible=yes ground=override, emit-body emitted the body at rc=0 with zero rounds; Critical 2: tampered draft emitted; Important 3: byte-distinct drafts minted an identical token key=0; Important 5: init --force wiped attestation=match), then re-driven after the fix and observed refusing. Each pin was then mutation-proven on a scratch copy: reverting the guard flips its row's operands RED (record-override rc 1->0, breadcrumb 1->0, eligible=no->yes, emit rc 1->0 emitting the body, tokens differ->identical). The blinded re-gate independently reproduced the same six mutations and confirmed each row goes RED only under its own.

step_3_5_gate.attempt_1: Important x2 — my token row was vacuous (two slugs -> two nonces forced the inequality regardless of the key; proven GREEN with the key fix reverted) and my it6 fixture comment was a documented_falsehood (inverted the reason for its own edit). Both fixed in 02cb1495.
step_3_5_gate.attempt_2: Important x1 — the read-boundary unbound-override guard had no pin; all 11 rows stayed green with it reverted while emit-body emitted arbitrary bytes. Fixed in fd07676c with the symmetric (2b) row.
step_3_5_gate.final: CLEAN — no Critical or Important findings. All six guarded rows proven non-vacuous individually; every diff-added claim verified true against HEAD; the shape matrix fails closed on every row; no legitimate lifecycle flow narrowed (verified live on the embed arm, which still legitimately omits --draft-file). One Suggestion parked (GATE-S2).
